fields.name: PWRCTRL
fields.description: SDMMC state control bits These bits can only be written when the SDMMC is not in the power-on state (PWRCTRL different from 11). These bits are used to define the functional state of the SDMMC signals: When written 00, power-off: the SDMMC is disabled and the clock to the card is stopped, SDMMC_D[7:0], SDMMC_CMD and SDMMC_CK are driven high. Any further write is ignored, PWRCTRL value keeps 11.

fields.name: VSWITCH
fields.description: Voltage switch sequence start This bit is used to start the timing critical section of the voltage switch sequence:

fields.name: VSWITCHEN
fields.description: Voltage switch procedure enable This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). This bit is used to stop the SDMMC_CK after the voltage switch command response:

fields.name: DIRPOL
fields.description: Data and command direction signals polarity selection This bit can only be written when the SDMMC is in the power-off state (PWRCTRL = 00).

fields.name: CLKDIV
fields.description: Clock divide factor This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0). This field defines the divide factor between the input clock (sdmmc_ker_ck) and the output clock (SDMMC_CK): SDMMC_CK frequency = sdmmc_ker_ck / [2 * CLKDIV]. 0x0XX: etc.. 0xXXX: etc..

fields.name: PWRSAV
fields.description: Power saving configuration bit This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0) For power saving, the SDMMC_CK clock output can be disabled when the bus is idle by setting PWRSAV:

fields.name: WIDBUS
fields.description: Wide bus mode enable bit This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0)

fields.name: NEGEDGE
fields.description: SDMMC_CK dephasing selection bit for data and command This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0). When clock division = 1 (CLKDIV = 0), this bit has no effect. Data and Command change on SDMMC_CK falling edge. Command and data changed on the sdmmc_ker_ck falling edge succeeding the rising edge of SDMMC_CK. SDMMC_CK edge occurs on sdmmc_ker_ck rising edge. When clock division >1 (CLKDIV > 0) & DDR = 1: Command changed on the sdmmc_ker_ck falling edge succeeding the rising edge of SDMMC_CK. Data changed on the sdmmc_ker_ck falling edge succeeding a SDMMC_CK edge. SDMMC_CK edge occurs on sdmmc_ker_ck rising edge. Command and data changed on the same sdmmc_ker_ck rising edge generating the SDMMC_CK falling edge. When clock division >1 (CLKDIV > 0) & DDR = 1: Command changed on the same sdmmc_ker_ck rising edge generating the SDMMC_CK falling edge. Data changed on the SDMMC_CK falling edge succeeding a SDMMC_CK edge. SDMMC_CK edge occurs on sdmmc_ker_ck rising edge.

fields.name: HWFC_EN
fields.description: Hardware flow control enable This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0) When Hardware flow control is enabled, the meaning of the TXFIFOE and RXFIFOF flags change, see SDMMC status register definition in Section 58.10.11.

fields.name: DDR
fields.description: Data rate signaling selection This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0) DDR rate must only be selected with 4-bit or 8-bit wide bus mode. (WIDBUS > 00). DDR = 1 has no effect when WIDBUS = 00 (1-bit wide bus). DDR rate must only be selected with clock division >1. (CLKDIV > 0)

fields.name: BUSSPEED
fields.description: Bus speed for selection of SDMMC operating modes This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0)

fields.name: SELCLKRX
fields.description: Receive clock selection These bits can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0)

fields.name: CMDARG
fields.description: Command argument These bits can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). Command argument sent to a card as part of a command message. If a command contains an argument, it must be loaded into this register before writing a command to the command register.

fields.name: CMDINDEX
fields.description: Command index This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). The command index is sent to the card as part of a command message.

fields.name: CMDTRANS
fields.description: The CPSM treats the command as a data transfer command, stops the interrupt period, and signals DataEnable to the DPSM This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). If this bit is set, the CPSM issues an end of interrupt period and issues DataEnable signal to the DPSM when the command is sent.

fields.name: CMDSTOP
fields.description: The CPSM treats the command as a Stop Transmission command and signals abort to the DPSM This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). If this bit is set, the CPSM issues the abort signal to the DPSM when the command is sent.

fields.name: WAITRESP
fields.description: Wait for response bits This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). They are used to configure whether the CPSM is to wait for a response, and if yes, which kind of response.

fields.name: WAITINT
fields.description: CPSM waits for interrupt request If this bit is set, the CPSM disables command timeout and waits for an card interrupt request (Response). If this bit is cleared in the CPSM Wait state, it causes the abort of the interrupt mode.

fields.name: WAITPEND
fields.description: CPSM waits for end of data transfer (CmdPend internal signal) from DPSM This bit when set, the CPSM waits for the end of data transfer trigger before it starts sending a command. WAITPEND is only taken into account when DTMODE = e.MMC stream data transfer, WIDBUS = 1-bit wide bus mode, DPSMACT = 1 and DTDIR = from host to card.

fields.name: CPSMEN
fields.description: Command path state machine (CPSM) enable bit This bit is written 1 by firmware, and cleared by hardware when the CPSM enters the Idle state. If this bit is set, the CPSM is enabled. When DTEN = 1, no command is transfered nor boot procedure is started. CPSMEN is cleared to 0. During Read Wait with SDMMC_CK stopped no command is sent and CPSMEN is kept 0.

fields.name: DTHOLD
fields.description: Hold new data block transmission and reception in the DPSM If this bit is set, the DPSM does not move from the Wait_S state to the Send state or from the Wait_R state to the Receive state.

fields.name: BOOTMODE
fields.description: Select the boot mode procedure to be used This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0)

fields.name: BOOTEN
fields.description: Enable boot mode procedure

fields.name: CMDSUSPEND
fields.description: The CPSM treats the command as a Suspend or Resume command and signals interrupt period start/end This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). CMDSUSPEND = 1 and CMDTRANS = 0 Suspend command, start interrupt period when response bit BS=0. CMDSUSPEND = 1 and CMDTRANS = 1 Resume command with data, end interrupt period when response bit DF=1.

fields.name: RESPCMD
fields.description: Response command index Read-only bit field. Contains the command index of the last command response received.

fields.name: CARDSTATUS
fields.description: Card status according table below See Table 444.

fields.name: CARDSTATUS
fields.description: Card status according table below See Table 444.

fields.name: CARDSTATUS
fields.description: Card status according table below See Table 444.

fields.name: CARDSTATUS
fields.description: Card status according table below See Table 444.

fields.name: DATATIME
fields.description: Data and R1b busy timeout period This bit can only be written when the CPSM and DPSM are not active (CPSMACT = 0 and DPSMACT = 0). Data and R1b busy timeout period expressed in card bus clock periods.

fields.name: DATALENGTH
fields.description: Data length value This register can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0). Number of data bytes to be transferred. When DDR = 1 DATALENGTH is truncated to a multiple of 2. (The last odd byte is not transfered) When DATALENGTH = 0 no data are transfered, when requested by a CPSMEN and CMDTRANS = 1 also no command is transfered. DTEN and CPSMEN are cleared to 0.

fields.name: DTEN
fields.description: Data transfer enable bit This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0). This bit is cleared by Hardware when data transfer completes. This bit must only be used to transfer data when no associated data transfer command is used, i.e. must not be used with SD or e.MMC cards.

fields.name: DTDIR
fields.description: Data transfer direction selection This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: DTMODE
fields.description: Data transfer mode selection This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: DBLOCKSIZE
fields.description: Data block size This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0). Define the data block length when the block data transfer mode is selected: When DATALENGTH is not a multiple of DBLOCKSIZE, the transfered data is truncated at a multiple of DBLOCKSIZE. (None of the remaining data are transfered.) When DDR = 1, DBLOCKSIZE = 0000 must not be used. (No data are transfered)

fields.name: RWSTART
fields.description: Read Wait start If this bit is set, Read Wait operation starts.

fields.name: RWSTOP
fields.description: Read Wait stop This bit is written by firmware and auto cleared by hardware when the DPSM moves from the R_W state to the Wait_R or Idle state.

fields.name: RWMOD
fields.description: Read Wait mode This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: SDIOEN
fields.description: SD I/O interrupt enable functions This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0). If this bit is set, the DPSM enables the SD I/O card specific interrupt operation.

fields.name: BOOTACKEN
fields.description: Enable the reception of the boot acknowledgment This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: FIFORST
fields.description: FIFO reset, flushes any remaining data This bit can only be written by firmware when IDMAEN= 0 and DPSM is active (DPSMACT = 1). This bit only takes effect when a transfer error or transfer hold occurs.

fields.name: DATACOUNT
fields.description: Data count value When read, the number of remaining data bytes to be transferred is returned. Write has no effect.

fields.name: CCRCFAIL
fields.description: Command response received (CRC check failed) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DCRCFAIL
fields.description: Data block sent/received (CRC check failed) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: CTIMEOUT
fields.description: Command response timeout Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR. The Command Timeout period has a fixed value of 64 SDMMC_CK clock periods.

fields.name: DTIMEOUT
fields.description: Data timeout Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: TXUNDERR
fields.description: Transmit FIFO underrun error (masked by hardware when IDMA is enabled) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: RXOVERR
fields.description: Received FIFO overrun error (masked by hardware when IDMA is enabled) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: CMDREND
fields.description: Command response received (CRC check passed, or no CRC) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: CMDSENT
fields.description: Command sent (no response required) Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DATAEND
fields.description: Data transfer ended correctly DATAEND is set if data counter DATACOUNT is zero and no errors occur, and no transmit data transfer hold. Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DHOLD
fields.description: Data transfer Hold Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DBCKEND
fields.description: Data block sent/received DBCKEND is set when: - CRC check passed and DPSM moves to the R_W state or - IDMAEN = 0 and transmit data transfer hold and DATACOUNT >0 and DPSM moves to Wait_S. Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DABORT
fields.description: Data transfer aborted by CMD12 Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: DPSMACT
fields.description: Data path state machine active, i.e. not in Idle state This is a hardware status flag only, does not generate an interrupt.

fields.name: CPSMACT
fields.description: Command path state machine active, i.e. not in Idle state This is a hardware status flag only, does not generate an interrupt.

fields.name: TXFIFOHE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO half empty At least half the number of words can be written into the FIFO. This bit is cleared when the FIFO becomes half+1 full.

fields.name: RXFIFOHF
fields.description: Receive FIFO half full There are at least half the number of words in the FIFO. This bit is cleared when the FIFO becomes half+1 empty.

fields.name: TXFIFOF
fields.description: Transmit FIFO full This is a hardware status flag only, does not generate an interrupt. This bit is cleared when one FIFO location becomes empty.

fields.name: RXFIFOF
fields.description: Receive FIFO full This bit is cleared when one FIFO location becomes empty.

fields.name: TXFIFOE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty This bit is cleared when one FIFO location becomes full.

fields.name: RXFIFOE
fields.description: Receive FIFO empty This is a hardware status flag only, does not generate an interrupt. This bit is cleared when one FIFO location becomes full.

fields.name: BUSYD0
fields.description: Inverted value of SDMMC_D0 line (Busy), sampled at the end of a CMD response and a second time 2 SDMMC_CK cycles after the CMD response This bit is reset to not busy when the SDMMCD0 line changes from busy to not busy. This bit does not signal busy due to data transfer. This is a hardware status flag only, it does not generate an interrupt.

fields.name: BUSYD0END
fields.description: end of SDMMC_D0 Busy following a CMD response detected This indicates only end of busy following a CMD response. This bit does not signal busy due to data transfer. Interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: SDIOIT
fields.description: SDIO interrupt received The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: ACKFAIL
fields.description: Boot acknowledgment received (boot acknowledgment check fail) The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: ACKTIMEOUT
fields.description: Boot acknowledgment timeout The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: VSWEND
fields.description: Voltage switch critical timing section completion The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: CKSTOP
fields.description: SDMMC_CK stopped in Voltage switch procedure The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: IDMATE
fields.description: IDMA transfer error The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: IDMABTC
fields.description: IDMA buffer transfer complete The interrupt flag is cleared by writing corresponding interrupt clear bit in SDMMC_ICR.

fields.name: CCRCFAILC
fields.description: CCRCFAIL flag clear bit Set by software to clear the CCRCFAIL flag.

fields.name: DCRCFAILC
fields.description: DCRCFAIL flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DCRCFAIL flag.

fields.name: CTIMEOUTC
fields.description: CTIMEOUT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the CTIMEOUT flag.

fields.name: DTIMEOUTC
fields.description: DTIMEOUT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DTIMEOUT flag.

fields.name: TXUNDERRC
fields.description: TXUNDERR flag clear bit Set by software to clear TXUNDERR flag.

fields.name: RXOVERRC
fields.description: RXOVERR flag clear bit Set by software to clear the RXOVERR flag.

fields.name: CMDRENDC
fields.description: CMDREND flag clear bit Set by software to clear the CMDREND flag.

fields.name: CMDSENTC
fields.description: CMDSENT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the CMDSENT flag.

fields.name: DATAENDC
fields.description: DATAEND flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DATAEND flag.

fields.name: DHOLDC
fields.description: DHOLD flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DHOLD flag.

fields.name: DBCKENDC
fields.description: DBCKEND flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DBCKEND flag.

fields.name: DABORTC
fields.description: DABORT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the DABORT flag.

fields.name: BUSYD0ENDC
fields.description: BUSYD0END flag clear bit Set by software to clear the BUSYD0END flag.

fields.name: SDIOITC
fields.description: SDIOIT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the SDIOIT flag.

fields.name: ACKFAILC
fields.description: ACKFAIL flag clear bit Set by software to clear the ACKFAIL flag.

fields.name: ACKTIMEOUTC
fields.description: ACKTIMEOUT flag clear bit Set by software to clear the ACKTIMEOUT flag.

fields.name: VSWENDC
fields.description: VSWEND flag clear bit Set by software to clear the VSWEND flag.

fields.name: CKSTOPC
fields.description: CKSTOP flag clear bit Set by software to clear the CKSTOP flag.

fields.name: IDMATEC
fields.description: IDMA transfer error clear bit Set by software to clear the IDMATE flag.

fields.name: IDMABTCC
fields.description: IDMA buffer transfer complete clear bit Set by software to clear the IDMABTC flag.

fields.name: CCRCFAILIE
fields.description: Command CRC fail interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by command CRC failure.

fields.name: DCRCFAILIE
fields.description: Data CRC fail interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by data CRC failure.

fields.name: CTIMEOUTIE
fields.description: Command timeout interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by command timeout.

fields.name: DTIMEOUTIE
fields.description: Data timeout interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by data timeout.

fields.name: TXUNDERRIE
fields.description: Tx FIFO underrun error interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Tx FIFO underrun error.

fields.name: RXOVERRIE
fields.description: Rx FIFO overrun error interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Rx FIFO overrun error.

fields.name: CMDRENDIE
fields.description: Command response received interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by receiving command response.

fields.name: CMDSENTIE
fields.description: Command sent interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by sending command.

fields.name: DATAENDIE
fields.description: Data end interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by data end.

fields.name: DHOLDIE
fields.description: Data hold interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the interrupt generated when sending new data is hold in the DPSM Wait_S state.

fields.name: DBCKENDIE
fields.description: Data block end interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by data block end.

fields.name: DABORTIE
fields.description: Data transfer aborted interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by a data transfer being aborted.

fields.name: TXFIFOHEIE
fields.description: Tx FIFO half empty interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Tx FIFO half empty.

fields.name: RXFIFOHFIE
fields.description: Rx FIFO half full interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Rx FIFO half full.

fields.name: RXFIFOFIE
fields.description: Rx FIFO full interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Rx FIFO full.

fields.name: TXFIFOEIE
fields.description: Tx FIFO empty interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Tx FIFO empty.

fields.name: BUSYD0ENDIE
fields.description: BUSYD0END interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the interrupt generated when SDMMC_D0 signal changes from busy to NOT busy following a CMD response.

fields.name: SDIOITIE
fields.description: SDIO mode interrupt received interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the interrupt generated when receiving the SDIO mode interrupt.

fields.name: ACKFAILIE
fields.description: Acknowledgment Fail interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by acknowledgment Fail.

fields.name: ACKTIMEOUTIE
fields.description: Acknowledgment timeout interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by acknowledgment timeout.

fields.name: VSWENDIE
fields.description: Voltage switch critical timing section completion interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the interrupt generated when voltage switch critical timing section completion.

fields.name: CKSTOPIE
fields.description: Voltage Switch clock stopped interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by Voltage Switch clock stopped.

fields.name: IDMABTCIE
fields.description: IDMA buffer transfer complete interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the interrupt generated when the IDMA has transferred all data belonging to a memory buffer.

fields.name: ACKTIME
fields.description: Boot acknowledgment timeout period This bit can only be written by firmware when CPSM is disabled (CPSMEN = 0). Boot acknowledgment timeout period expressed in card bus clock periods.

fields.name: IDMAEN
fields.description: IDMA enable This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: IDMABMODE
fields.description: Buffer mode selection This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: IDMABNDT
fields.description: Number of bytes per buffer This 12-bit value must be multiplied by 8 to get the size of the buffer in 32-bit words and by 32 to get the size of the buffer in bytes. Example: IDMABNDT = 0x001: buffer size = 8 words = 32 bytes. Example: IDMABNDT = 0x800: buffer size = 16384 words = 64 Kbyte. These bits can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

registers.name: IDMABASER
registers.description: SDMMC IDMA buffer base address register

fields.name: IDMABASE
fields.description: Buffer memory base address bits [31:2], must be word aligned (bit [1:0] are always 0 and read only) This register can be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0), and can dynamically be written by firmware when DPSM active (DPSMACT = 1).

registers.name: IDMALAR
registers.description: SDMMC IDMA linked list address register

fields.name: IDMALA
fields.description: Word aligned linked list item address offset Linked list item offset pointer to the base of the next linked list item structure. Linked list item base address is IDMABA + IDMALA. These bits can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: ABR
fields.description: Acknowledge linked list buffer ready This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0). This bit is not taken into account when starting the first linked list buffer from the software programmed register information. ABR is only taken into account on subsequent loaded linked list items.

fields.name: ULS
fields.description: Update SDMMC_IDMABSIZE from the next linked list when in linked list mode (SDMMC_IDMACTRLR.IDMABMODE select linked list mode and ULA = 1) This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: ULA
fields.description: Update SDMMC_IDMALAR from linked list when in linked list mode (SDMMC_IDMACTRLR.IDMABMODE select linked list mode) This bit can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

registers.name: IDMABAR
registers.description: SDMMC IDMA linked list memory base register

fields.name: IDMABA
fields.description: Word aligned Linked list memory base address Linked list memory base pointer. These bits can only be written by firmware when DPSM is inactive (DPSMACT = 0).

fields.name: FIFODATA
fields.description: Receive and transmit FIFO data This register can only be read or written by firmware when the DPSM is active (DPSMACT = 1). The FIFO data occupies 16 entries of 32-bit words.
